processes.issue: All GPU parts must be returned to Lenovo US through a special return process:
processes.process:
All GPU parts must be returned to Lenovo US through a special return process, even if the part is marked as non’returnable or no RMA line appears in the work order. It is the full responsibility of RTS to ensure the SSR is aware of this procedure.
I currently have a serious escalation regarding a case where a team member mistakenly informed the SSR that the part did not need to be returned. This is urgent and needs immediate attention to prevent further issues.

Here is a gentle reminder for some GPU scenarios:

Kindly ensure that all team members:

Select the RTS Logo in the Selected Country field.

Start using the new utilization codes immediately.







As highlighted by Sherif, we are required to update our reports retroactively starting from May 1, 2026, using the new ARP codes. This will ensure that our May utilization is accurately reflected.



Please note the following guidelines:

Service Detail Selection: “Clnt_Adv/RelationMgt” is the only recoverable item.

Activity Description (Activity Desc): This field is mandatory. Please provide a clear, concise, and to-the-point note describing the activity performed.

Meetings:  Use “Other (Exception)” for standard meetings.

If the meeting is related to a recoverable or productive activity, select the appropriate code based on the scope of work.



LINK for the recording.







Thank you for your cooperation and attention to these updates.

I would like to remind and refresh everyone that whenever a case is triggered in the Entitlement queue, it is essential to first verify, using the Lenovo Warranty Lookup tool, whether the machine is covered under warranty and to identify the type of contract whether it falls under Lenovo’s standard coverage (IOL/IOR) or a growth contract.

In some rare instances, cases may be triggered and remain pending in the EEH queue (MSD or OCPM), despite being covered by an active warranty or onsite contract. Therefore, before taking any action whether sending an EEH template or proceeding with technical handling, it is mandatory to check the machine’s entitlement status using the Lenovo Warranty Lookup tool.

Thank you very much for your understanding and collaboration.

CMOS batteries are consumables and therefore not covered by any SLAs.
For environmental and cost reasons, they will only be shipped NBD from the NL DC from now on.
Please adjust your ordering behaviors accordingly.

:bell: Segnalazione di massima importanza:

Per il cliente :bell: Confindustria Verona, è fondamentale prestare la massima attenzione a quanto richiesto da SDM nella mail in calce.

Ho già segnalato che va applicata la parte relativa al ruolo del RTS.

Per quanto riguarda gli altri team, è importante che vengano coinvolti secondo quanto indicato.

==============================
:bell: Richiesta SDM:

Please acknowledge and ensure with the team the below points agreed with Confindustria Verona from now on, so to close the loop on client dissatisfaction created.


Nessuno interverrà più nel DC finchè non si riceveranno tutte le autorizzazioni del caso. L’importante è ricevere il corretto contatto di riferimento durante apertura ticket



Attività che comportano anche il minimo rischio per la produzione verranno pianificate fuori orario di lavoro e fuori produzione
Il tecnico coinvolto nella gestione degli ultimi tickets, probabilmente diventato il capro espiatorio degli errori anche di altri, verrà sostituito da un altra persona che dovrà confrontarsi con il supporto remoto per assicurare che l’action plan da applicare sia chiaro e senza alcun rischio. Se fattibile e necessario, eventuali sessioni remote tra il tecnico onsite e i livelli superiori di supporto verranno effettuate on demand


Cercare di prevedere interventi on site già con il componente sostitutivo al seguito, in caso di valido e concreto sospetto di malfunzionamento di una specifica parte, in modo da poter completare la riparazione in un unico intervento
Ho lasciato i miei riferimenti come punto di escalation per eventuali disguidi o criticità, in modo da coordinare e dare il giusto focus ove necessario anche con ATS

Vi ringrazio moltissimo per la vostra eccezionale collaborazione, come sempre.
=============================

processes.issue: EEH Process mainly for OSS Countries (Spain, Italy and France) - Turkey CC FYA - Rest FYI
processes.process:
FYI

Hello Team,

Please review the updated process below. This process is primarily intended for OSS teams, and the step regarding RTS responsibilities during dispatch applies to all RTS teams.

Ensure that this information is shared with all team members and is implemented from now on, we will go through the same during our meeting today.

CC Team – Turkish Team 

@Basem El-Nahas

@Ahmed Ismail

 - Please take this with your teams.
When handling EEH activities for Turkey, please begin following the process immediately.

Updated Process After Receiving a Signed T&M Voucher

1. Submission of Signed Order Confirmations

Once the customer signs the T&M voucher (order confirmation),
send it directly to the Functional ID:


Lenovo.ISG.EMEA.TLS.Delivery@ibm.com

Do not send it to Market CSMs anymore. This is a new requirement for EEH/1SS markets managing the EEH task, RTS, and SSR activities.

Buongiorno a tutti,
Just a gentle  refresh :
For GMB cases, we do not create onsite WO, sometimes it's not mentioned in the company name GBM, so we have to check the emails:
Once its @GBM so it's GBM company and we cannot send SSR, we create CRU only.
Example : 3000679399 
Many thanks for your cooperation and outstanding support
===============

Hello Team,
Following further clarification from DPE, I would like to provide an update:
•	The execution of the tool is the customer’s responsibility
•	It is not performed by IBM SSR anymore.
Premier and ATS/PE will be updated accordingly.
All other points shared in the previous communication remain valid and unchanged.
Please ensure that this is taken into consideration moving forward.
Thak you!

Dear colleagues,

Regarding the Growth Program, I would like to inform you that we requested a list of Italian clients with Growth Contracts so that we could be alerted when they open cases and apply the appropriate process. Unfortunately, we have received confirmation from Asen that no such list of IBM Growth clients is available.

Therefore, I urge you to follow the process outlined below moving forward:

As usual, retrieve the serial number and enter it into the Lenovo Warranty Lookup Tool.
Please pay close attention whenever you encounter “IBMPWTechInstall” in the results, as this indicates a Growth Contract. When this appears, proceed as follows:


Keep the MSD open for parts ordering and case escalation if necessary.



Open a case in CSP to report your time and create an onsite work order (refer to the attached guidance).



Use Archive Explorer for the emailing system: https://ecurep.mainz.de.ibm.com/ae5/



For now, you may ask the customer to upload logs to Lenovo LSC and parse logs from there. We are planning to introduce our own log parsing tool and will keep you updated once it is available.


For example, I recently noted that SEEWEB is one of the largest IBM Italian clients under the Growth Program. If you receive a new case from SEEWEB, it will most likely be covered by a Growth Contract.
==============================

processes.issue: Handling of Expired Premier / ThinkAgile Contracts – Process Alignment:
processes.process:
Please find below the agreed process for handling any expired Premier or ThinkAgile cases that you may receive.

Teams operating as OSS are expected to apply this process from now on. For all other teams, the CC teams will apply the same actions accordingly.

Special thanks to 

@George George Samih Victor Michael

 for sharing the case scenario below, which helped drive alignment and establish a consistent approach for handling similar cases in the future.

I will share the process directly with my OSS teams in Spain, Italy, and France and rest of teams FTI.

For the remaining countries, I believe the new process should be communicated to all CC teams in the other countries through the respective CSMs.

Below is the proposed approach for how RTS/CC should handle cases involving expired contracts (Premier or ThinkAgile):

Handling of Expired Premier / ThinkAgile Contract Cases

For Premier or ThinkAgile cases where the contract has expired, IBM will inform the customer that the serial number is out of warranty.

The customer will be referred to the Lenovo Sales team for contract renewal.

IBM will notify the assigned CSM and SDM via email, including the customer name and case number.

IBM will not share the Time & Materials (T&M) document.

IBM will not wait for customer confirmation and will proceed with case cancellation accordingly.

Lenovo W3 Publisher:

Whenever you need to access a specific process, especially with the frequent updates, modifications, and new procedures we introduce, make sure to visit our publisher page:


https://w3.ibm.com/w3publisher/lenovo-isg


There is a dedicated tab for all process documentation, and I strongly encourage everyone to check there first whenever you cannot find a certain process.
If you still cannot locate a process or if you need support in documenting a new one, please feel free to reach out. I’m happy to help ensure the process is properly added and updated on the page.

processes.issue: How to identify whether WO to be Break & Fix or not:
processes.process:
How to identify whether WO to be Break & Fix or not:

go onsite and collect logs, go onsite and take pictures, go onsite and upgrade FW, go onsite for environmental check and take a look, check cables, clean dust without any part to replace etc



RTS ops to communicate via mail with CSM to check with him to ask aaproval to SDM to confirm NON B&F 

Once NON B&F Labo is approved RTS to select in WO ( Break and Fix)= NO :



Whenever a WO is created as labor’only for a non–Break & Fix (Non’B&F) activity—such as in the case we are discussing in this email thread—the B&F activity flag must be set to “No” instead of “Yes.”


This ensures the WO is correctly identified as a Non’B&F activity, which will follow a different billing method.

Please remind your teams accordingly.
If you have any concerns or would like to discuss this further, we can address it during our call today or on Monday.

*First Method : Using the FFDC Log (XCC) :
1.Open the FFDC service data log using Universal Parser in LSC .
- Search for dmdb_pci_dumper and locate:
> Storage → specify the Drive Model Number (Name) : SSDSC2KB960GZL
> Wear Report → specify the TBW 
2. Google the drives specs of the Drive Model to identify the drive Marketing Family Name.
3. Once identified the Marketing Name, we locate its Product Guide page.
Google LenovoPress S4520
4. Find the “Technical specifications” table > Endurance (total bytes written, TBW) value supported by Lenovo.
>Finally compare TBW supported and the TBW in the FFDC log.
==============
 

*Second Method : Using OneCLI or IBM/Lenovo Portable DSA (System x Servers) :
Open the log and locate SSD section.
Record: Number of bytes written, Bytes supported by warranty (TBW)
Compare values: Bytes Written ≤ Bytes Supported → covered; otherwise, out of warranty.
==============


*Third Method : Lenovo Wear Gauge Utility (All platforms)
Do NOT use Wear Gauge v10.2.0 or older
Check "Warranty Exceed" :Yes or No

Created by AbdelWadoud / 09 April 2026

This is a quick reminder regarding key points for Queue Handling:

1. Technical Queue
The Queue Watcher is responsible for monitoring both MSD and OCPM queues until 19:00 Cairo Time, and assigning calls according to the applicable SLA.
Scenario: Last Hour (18:00–19:00 Cairo Time)
• For 7x24 or 11x5 SLA:
 The Queue Watcher should assign the case immediately.
• For 8x17 SLA (IOL or IOR):
 The Queue Watcher should CC the case, save it, and leave it to be handled by the team the following morning.
Important Notes
We do not handle the following machine types:
 

    7159 – Handled by UK Team

    4587 (D1212) – Handled by Storage Team

As communicated by Sherif, please CC these cases, and Ahmed El Sakka’s team will handle Middle East cases for these models.
 

    DE/DM Storage Cases for Gulf countries – Handled by the Spanish Team

=============================================================================================

2. EEH Queue
The Queue Watcher must monitor the Entitlement Queue (ITMELEEH) in OCPM until 19:00 Cairo Time.
Scenario: Last Two Hours (17:00–19:00 Cairo Time)
 

    The Queue Watcher should continuously monitor the OCPM EEH (ITMELEEH) queue.

    Check whether the machine is covered under warranty/SLA.

If the machine is uncovered:
 

    17:00–18:00 → Queue Watcher must AK the case and email the customer informing them the machine is OOW (Out of Warranty).

    18:00–19:00 → Leave the case to be handled the next morning.

If the machine is covered under 8x17 SLA:
 

    Case triggered 17:00–18:00 → Unlock and assign.

    Case triggered 18:00–19:00 → Unlock the case but leave it for next-day handling.

If the machine is covered under 7x24 or 11x5 SLA:
 

    Case triggered 17:00–19:00 → Unlock and assign immediately for handling.







If you have any concerns, comments, or need clarification, please let me know so we can schedule a meeting to address all points.
Many thanks for your usual collaboration and understanding.
=============================

Hello Italian Team,

In some rare instances, cases may be triggered and remain pending in the EEH queue (MSD or OCPM), despite being covered by an active warranty or onsite contract. Therefore, before taking any action whether sending an EEH template or proceeding with technical handling, it is mandatory to check the machine’s entitlement status using the Lenovo Warranty Lookup tool.

=============
Regarding the Growth Program, I would like to inform you that we requested a list of Italian clients with Growth Contracts so that we could be alerted when they open cases and apply the appropriate process. Unfortunately, we have received confirmation from Asen that no such list of IBM Growth clients is available.

Therefore, I urge you to follow the process outlined below moving forward:

As usual, retrieve the serial number and enter it into the Lenovo Warranty Lookup Tool.
Please pay close attention whenever you encounter “IBMPWTechInstall” in the results, as this indicates a Growth Contract. When this appears, proceed as follows:

 

    Keep the MSD open for parts ordering and case escalation if necessary.



 

    Open a case in CSP to report your time and create an onsite work order (refer to the attached guidance).



 

    Use Archive Explorer for the emailing system: https://ecurep.mainz.de.ibm.com/ae5/



 

    For now, you may ask the customer to upload logs to Lenovo LSC and parse logs from there. We are planning to introduce our own log parsing tool and will keep you updated once it is available.



For example, I recently noted that SEEWEB is one of the largest IBM Italian clients under the Growth Program. If you receive a new case from SEEWEB, it will most likely be covered by a Growth Contract.
==============================

Thank you very much for your understanding and collaboration.

 

Thanks!

processes.issue: Mandatory Step Before DIMM Replacement – Advanced Memory Test
processes.process:
Hello Team,
Following management direction, please note the updated guidance regarding DIMM replacement activities.
Advanced Memory Testing must always be recommended before proceeding with DIMM replacement.
This is now the standard approach, mainly to avoid unnecessary hardware replacement due to current supply constraints.
•	Only in cases where the customer explicitly refuses to perform the test, it may be skipped.
In such situations, the rationale must be clearly documented in the case.

Key points to keep in mind
•	The test should be requested systematically before DIMM replacement
It helps: Confirm the actual failing DIMM & Avoid unnecessary part replacement
•	If the test is not feasible (e.g., customer refusal or operational constraints), ensure: 
o	Proper justification is documented

Operational consideration
•	Please remember that the Advanced Memory Test:
Requires system reboot and downtime
Must be coordinated with the customer (maintenance window if needed)

Reference
•	LXPM Advanced Memory Test reduces DIMM errors - Lenovo ThinkSystem 
https://datacentersupport.lenovo.com/om/en/solutions/ht511056

•	How to run Advanced Memory Test using Lenovo XClarity Essentials OneCLI
https://support.lenovo.com/us/en/solutions/ht514081

Please make sure to apply this approach in all applicable cases going forward.
If you have any questions or need clarification, feel free to reach out.
Thank you!

processes.issue: New Process to control subsequent WOs:
processes.process:
Staffing: 

2 SME Cairo for Cairo supported markets: Ahmed El-Sakka (GPU/HPC), Mohamed Sherif (all other products)
2 SME Zagreb for Zagreb supported markets: Berislav Bevanda (GPU/HPC) Martin Pryds Christensen  (all other products)

Back up: 2 GPU/HPC SME together, 2 all other products SME together



Goal: 

ISG Standard : Review and challenge every 3rd(should we change to 2nd ?) and any further action plan need to get approval of RTS SME, Standard only in MSD. 4th WO and up review include RTS FLM approval



Review actions: 

Review previous action plans (WO#1, WO#2) and assess reason for failing - Address SSR(directly or thru CSM?) is AP failed is due to wrongly applied AP (ie wrong order of actions) and/or lack of structured feedback to L1/2/3

Review 3rd action plan and assess if action plan can resolve the BF cases, and if not address L1/L2 respectively L3 depending on the AP originator

Request the opening of a new/different case if action plan is:Not allowing the SSR to solve the BF

Is requesting to process PD actions on site (pull log instead of the client, take pictures, test configs,…)

Not related to the originally reported issue

Is addressing another issue

Fill OVD (or TBD worksheet) to gain Lenovo approval if new case open and follow up down to billing

SLA: Immediate when triggered by IBM L1/2 Agents having such a 3rd of more AP, this before launching next ONS WO



Volumes: to be checked with Alem to get vol of STD cases, reaching 3rd AP or more and weekly average

Incl all STD cases, as well the ones performed RTS wise by satellite team members/SSRs assigned to ISG RTS

Q4 2025 ONS Cases Volumes 3007 total , 2137 STD (73%) vs 834 PRE (27%)



Communication:

With L3: MS Teams channel on the Lenovo MS Teams instance/Internal IBM Slack channel



Documentation: 

SME need to apply comments/summarize their review on the MSD case

To be defined how to check if the WO have been reviewed vs not 

When new case is open (new BF issues, non-std BF actions), this need to be tracked through a specific file parallel to OVD



Outcomes/Benefits:

3rd WO and more are questioned to ensure Lenovo parts/L3 costs as well as IBM ON costs are limited

3rd WO and more unrelated to previous ones but still BF are moved to new case

3rd WO and more with actions pointing at non-std BF work are moved to new cases leading to hourly billing



Less WOs less parts, lower cost for Lenovo

Less WOs less labor, lower cost for IBM

Quicker service and better customer experience, better for the end customers



Open questions: 

How to cover 24/7 for STD

How to cover Premier cases —> IBM team as no access to Premier MSD, WO landing directly on CC/CSP, one option to have CSP WO captured retroactively after 2nd failed? 





Asen Donchev

EMEA Lenovo RTS Leader

Global Lenovo Project Management Office(PMO) 

IBM Systems 
Mobile: +359 88 2367299
Email: 

asen.donchev@bg.ibm.com

processes.issue: ONS Break & Fix vs Non’Break & Fix Activities
processes.process:
All, when thinking about non–break-fix activities, consider them as scenarios where we go onsite without an acceptable level of confidence that the visit will resolve the issue. For example:

Onsite visits to replace faulty parts – Based on diagnostics, we are highly confident the issue will be resolved. Some risk exists (e.g., DOA parts or misdiagnosis), but it is low, understandable, and acceptable. This is BAU and as it is today

Diagnostics/exploratory visits with low to zero confidence – These include collecting logs, taking pictures, or measuring environmental conditions. These are information-gathering visits where we have little to no confidence that the issue will be resolved. These are clear scenarios we should invoice Lenovo for separately. What we need is an early way to identify such APs, especially when they are created by ATS or L3, and to convert any second or consecutive visit for the same mtsn into a new case (not a new WO). 

Preventive/configuration actions (medium confidence) – These include firmware updates, reseating or swapping parts, and correcting cabling or setup. These scenarios fall in the middle; sometimes they resolve the issue, and sometimes they do not. They are the hardest to address. Ideally, if the first onsite visit fails, we should identify any second or consecutive onsite WO for the same mtsn and convert it into a new case (not a new WO).

I trust you can add more scenarios which fail in one of the categories mentioned above. The reason behind this is that Youssef conducted an analysis of labor’only Work Orders (WOs) across the top 5 EMEA countries, and the findings showed that 14% of dispatched labor’only WOs were incorrectly created as B&F instead of Non’B&F.

During our call, we will cover the following points:

What is the difference between B&F and Non’B&F from a support and billing perspective?

How can we identify Non’B&F cases early, especially when the action plan does not fall under standard B&F activities?

How do we correctly identify and classify such WOs?

When should we open a separate new case?

Can we mix B&F and Non’B&F WOs within the same case?

If the first WO is created as B&F, can a subsequent WO in the same case be created as Non’B&F, and vice versa?

What is expected from CSM? 

Lenovo SDM OVD.

IBM CSM to confirm TC & follow up with Lenovo billing once the case is closed.

processes.issue: OVD Process - ONS WO for CMOS Replacement

Proof of approval via email and the OVD file.

Not related to the originally reported issue

SLA: Immediate when triggered by IBM L1/2 Agents having such a 3rd of more AP, this before launching next ONS WO

processes.issue: Returning Heavy or Non’Trackable NTP Parts
processes.process:
If a customer expresses concerns or dissatisfaction about returning a Non’Tier Part (NTP)—such as UPS batteries or any other heavy or non’trackable components—we have an official process they must follow.

You can refer them to the Lenovo/IBM documentation here:
https://ibm.box.com/s/kaafc3yo7xmc2pkvd82e7t1ev0yx7ml9

See page 67: Ad Hoc Collection of Non’Return Parts (waste) – Heavy & Hazardous FRU Returns.( PDF attached )

According to this guideline:
If an EU customer requests that an IBM/Lenovo SSR remove a non’return, heavy, or hazardous part (such as old UPS batteries) from their site, the SSR must politely decline.
Instead, direct the customer to the European Advanced Recycling Network (EARN) page, where they can submit a Proforma Request for an accredited waste’management specialist to collect and dispose of the part safely:

ðŸ‘‰ 

https://tool.earn-service.com/lenovo/select

This ensures compliance with environmenta

Dears FYA :
For Gulf countries, if a case is in RPS Hold for more than 24 h, and we send a reminder to them, please add in CC The SDM Alaa Halim
ahalime@lenovo.com
Thanks
==========
